scenario: You have created an Azure Logic App that synchronizes data between Dataverse and a third-party web app. Your company has updated its security policies to require custom applications to be governed by Azure Managed Identity.
q: Which Power Platform user type should you create for the correct authentication of users of your logic application?
why: Create an Application user in Dataverse. An application user is a non-licensed, non-interactive identity linked to an Azure app registration / managed identity, and it is the correct user type for a service (such as an Azure Logic App governed by Azure Managed Identity) to authenticate and call Dataverse. Regular, Non-interactive, and Support are user access modes for human/system accounts and are not the identity used for a managed-identity governed application.
